rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, Heather Fong, Ernest Ferrando Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery, And For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 27, 2012, Line 17, Defendants City And County Of San Francisco, Heather Fong, Ernest Ferrando Motion To Compel Responses To Written Discovery, And For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions. Granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff must provide further responses without objections to all discovery by March 2, 2012. Plaintiff and/or her counsel shall pay $1400 in sanctions to the City by March 2, 2012. The request for terminating sanctions is denied. Plaintiff and her counsel shall immediately satisfy all outstanding sanction obligations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VATCHE CABAYAN M.D. VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion To Compel State Compesnsation Insurance Fund To Provude Furtehr Supplemental Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday February 27, 2012 line 2 - PLAINTIFF VATCHE CABAYAN M.D., VATCHE CABAYAN MEDICAL CORPORATION A CALIFORNIA (Amended) Motion To Compel State Compesnsation Insurance Fund To Provude Furtehr Supplemental Responses. Pro Tem Judge Rolando Pasquali, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion is denied as to requests 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 16, & 17 based upon both procedural and substantive grounds. Plaintiff's motion as to request 12 is denied solely upon procedural grounds. Plaintiff's Notice of Motion violates CRC 3.1110(b)(1) on ambiguity exists as to whether the correct hearing date was noticed (February 21, on page 2 of the Notice vs. Feb 27, in the caption). Neither defect was corrected within the time provided by Code of Civil Procedure §1005(b). As to request numbers 8, 9, 10, 11, 13, 14, 15, 16, & 17, the motion is also denied on substantive grounds; see Insurance Code §1877.4 and the declaration of Alameda County Deputy District Attorney Matthew Beltramo which states that there is "a law enforcement investigation of the medical practice of Vatche Cabayan, M.D." Responding party's objections pursuant to Insurance Code are sustained. The court finds that under Evidence Code §1040(b)(2) disclosure is against the public interest in that an ongoing law enforcement investigation constitutes a necessity for preserving the confidentiality of the information that outweighs the necessity for disclosure in the interest of justice. As to moving party's RICO argument, the court notes that the discovery request was served 6 days before the RICO Cross-Complaint was executed and both the response and supplemental responses pre-dated the filing of the RICO Cross-Complaint. Nothing in this ruling is intended to have any effect upon moving party's discovery rights in any other proceedings. No sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to lawyer@netwiz.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DEBBIE FINDLING et al VS. EDWARD STUART-PENROSE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Inspection Of 673 Kansas Street Pursuant To Ccp 2031.300 & Request For Sanctions; Memo Of P&A; Declaration Of Jannik Catalano
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday February 27, 2012 line 4 - CROSS COMPLAINANT HEATHER GIBBONS, EDWARD STURT-PENROSE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Inspection Of 673 Kansas Street Pursuant To Ccp 2031.300 & Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rolando Pasquali, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted as follows. Responding party waived objections by failing to timely object as provided by CCP 2031.210, et seq. Respondent is ordered to provide a response fully compliant with CCP §2031.210, et seq. within 15 days. Response to contain a statement as to whether entry is permitted within 21 days of the response date. If entry is not permitted because the tenants refuse to permit same, the response shall include all existing proof of reasonable, timely, and diligent efforts to obtain entry and of tenants' express refusal to permit same. If entry is permitted, the parties shall either mutually agree upon a date/time for entry and inspection, including duration of same or, at respondent's option, respondent may provide three alternative dates and times during which entry shall be permitted for a two hour period. If respondent elects to propose alternative dates: respondent will provide three separate dates, at least one of which shall be during regular business hours on a weekday absent a showing that a weekday poses an undue inconvenience or hardship upon the tenants; and the proposed dates must be not less than 5 nor more than 21 days from the date of the response. Unless the parties agree otherwise, moving party may enter with no more than 3 persons total. Respondent may have an equal number of persons present. No destructive testing is allowed. Sanctions are awarded in the sum of $900 (3 hours x $300 per hour) plus $40 filing fee. Total sanctions $940 awarded against responding parties and their attorneys jointly, payable within 30 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to lawyer@netwiz.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ECONOMY FOODS, INC A CORPORATION VS. SILVER LYNING INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DBA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moton For Assignment Of Rights Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday February 27, 2012 line 5 - PLAINTIFF ECONOMY FOODS, INC A CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Moton For Assignment Of Rights Restraining Order And Turnover Order. Pro Tem Judge Rolando Pasquali, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted in part and denied in part but ONLY as to defendant Silver Lyning, Inc., a Calif. Corporation dba SOFIA on 11th. While Plaintiff's complaint is captioned against multiple defendants, including Jeremy Bennett, individually and dba SOFIA on 11th, the default judgment does not include said defendant. Accordingly, that portion of the motion seeking assignment, a restraining order, and turn over from any party other than Silver Lyning, Inc. is denied. As to Silver Lyning, Inc., if Plaintiff supplies a supplemental declaration stating that defendants have failed to pay any monies voluntarily, have been uncooperative as alleged in its Memorandum of Points and Authorities, and that the full amount remains due on the money judgement as provided by CCP §708.510(c)(3) the court will grant those portions of the motion seeking Assignment of Rights and Turnover CCP §699.040(a) only. Issuance of a Restraining Order is conditioned upon Plaintiff first providing an undertaking pursuant to CCP §708.520(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to lawyer@netwiz.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY B. EPSTEIN VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER, GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Docs Located At The State'S Privilege Log
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday February 27, 2012 line 6 Part II - PLAINTIFF JERRY EPSTEIN, A. DOMS, CALIFORNIA FIRST LP's Motion To Compel Documents Located At The State'S Privilege Log. While counsel for respondent is correct in its contention that documents prepared by employees at the direction of counsel for the purpose of giving legal advice are privileged, Respondent's citation to O'Mary v. Mitsubishi Electronics America, Inc. (1997) 59 CA4th 563 is noteworthy in that, unlike the instant proceeding, the "purpose" of the communications in O'Mary was "undisputed" whereas a dispute exists here. Accordingly, Respondent shall augment the "Privilege Asserted" section of its privilege log by including information as to the purpose for the objected to communications in items 15, 72, 126, 143, 145, 158, 187, 192, 204. While augmentation shall not disclose the information which Respondent seeks to protect, it shall contain information similar to the supporting arguments presented by its counsel on pages 6-8 of its opposition papers. It appears to the court that the dominant purpose of the redacted portions of items 15 and 187 was to communicate inquiries to counsel and, as such, the communications are protected. Similarly, from the representations of counsel, it appears that items 72, 143, 145, 158, and the redacted portions of 126 and 204 pertain to communications to/from counsel and are protected. Respondent is directed to clarify its use of "relates to this litigation" relative to its description of item 192 in light of the date of the item when compared to the initiation of this litigation. Responding party shall produce the names of all recipients of all copies of e-mail transmissions. Propounding party is entitled to ascertain whether evidence of waiver exists within the meaning of Evidence Code §912. See, e.g. Merritt v. Superior Court (1970) 9 CA3d 721, 729. Regarding Metadata. Responding party shall produce all metadata for non-privileged documents only. As to BEG ATTACH and END ATTACH, Responding party does not object (except for cost) and all such data shall be produced at moving party's expense. Except as otherwise noted, compliance shall occur within 30 days. In all other respects, the motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to lawyer@netwiz.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY B. EPSTEIN VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER, GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Docs Located At The State'S Privilege Log; Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday February 27, 2012 line 6 Part 1 - PLAINTIFF JERRY EPSTEIN, A. DOMS, CALIFORNIA FIRST LP's Motion To Compel Documents Located At The State'S Privilege Log. Pro Tem Judge Rolando Pasquali, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court wishes to disclose that this temporary judge, is a social acquaintance of Niall McCarthy a partner in the Cotchett firm which is counsel of record for one of the parties in this action, though not a moving nor responding party to this motion. This temporary judge has in the past referred clients (including a very close friend of this temporary judge's spouse) to Mr. McCarthy on a protracted litigation matter that may still be pending. Mr. McCarthy has also referred prospective clients to this temporary judge in his private practice, both of which are situated in Burlingame, California. However, no referral fees have been paid, nor does this temporary judge believe that he has accepted as clients any persons referred to him by Mr. McCarthy. Accordingly, this temporary judge is not aware of any circumstance that might indicate the presence of a conflict or bias for or against either the Cochett firm or its client. Pursuant to its offer to do so, Respondent shall produce items 80, 81, 87, and non-redacted versions of items 201, 202, 203, 205, and 206 within 14 days. While Respondent is not required to provide a separate itemized privilege log for each discrete e-mail identified in its entire log, it shall amend its privilege log by listing each e-mail within the chain as a separate item as to the following log items 81, 84, 151, 173 and 174. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MIDLAND FUNDING, LLC VS. ANTHONY HARRIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday February 27, 2012 line 7 - PLAINTIFF MIDLAND FUNDING, LLC's Motion For Order Establishing Admissions And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rolando Pasquali, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied, no sanctions are awarded. Moving party failed to attach a copy of the Request for Admissions to its moving papers. Nowhere in the moving papers, nor in the declaration of David Snyder, is proof provided as to precisely what was served upon Defendant. The only document provided in the moving papers is a proof of service stating "PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION TO DEFENDANT, SET NO. ONE,"without a copy of the request constituting part of the moving papers. The court cannot speculate that the Requests for Admission complied with Code of Civil Procedure §2033.010; nor can the court speculate that the requests were directed to Anthony Harris within the meaning of Code of Civil Procedure §2033.280 since the procedure for deeming matters admitted requires proof that "the party to whom the requests for admission are directed" has failed to respond.Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to lawyer@netwiz.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL MELLANDER VS. MOTOROLA, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Motorola Inc. And Motorla Mobility Inc.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday February 27, 2012 line 9 - Defendants Motorola Inc. And Motorla Mobility Inc.'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Rolando Pasquali, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge.The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants Motorola and Motorola Mobility's motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory #4, plaintiff's objections are overruled. The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory #5. Special Interrogatory number 5 seeks mental health information not placed in issue at the time that the interrogatory was propounded. Moving parties papers focus upon this discovery being propounded because of the allegations in a judicial council form complaint. The stated reason for seeking mental health care information, i.e. "to evaluate whether Plaintiff is of sound mind and not at fault for his own injury" is speculative based upon the pleadings and state of discovery in existence at the time that the special interrogatory was propounded. See Davis v. Superior Court (1992) 7 CalApp4th 1008, 1016-17 and In re Lifschutz (1970) 2 Cal.3d 415, 435. The court notes that, one month after the discovery was propounded, an amended complaint was filed. Therefore, this ruling is without prejudice to discovery being re-propounded in conformity with the issues placed in controversy at the time that a particular item of discovery is propounded. Moving party's papers violate CRC 3.1345(c)(2). The bilateral requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to lawyer@netwiz.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: IRA I BLOOM (PROVIDE ACCESS)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition For Order Entitlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 27, 2012, Line 7, PETITIONER IRA BLOOM Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition For Order Entitlement. Hearing required. The court did not receive any courtesy copies for this motion. The moving party must bring a courtesy copy of the proof of service to the hearing. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CAI XIAO SHENG VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 27, 2012, Line 8, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Continued to March 20, 2012 per the request in defendant's notice of non-opposition to enable the parties to address the issue of the standing of plaintiff Kaylee Choy. Defendant's notice of non-opposition will be treated as an amendment to its motion to strike as to this issue and plaintiffs are given leave to file opposition papers on all issues raised by the motion to strike, including the standing of Kaylee Choy, by March 8 and defendant is given leave to file further reply papers by March 14. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CAI XIAO SHENG VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al

rulings.Case Title: THOMPSON TECHNOLOGY INDUSTRIES VS. STELLAR ENERGY SOLUTIONS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Complaint; Affidavit; Memo Of P & A And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 27, 2012, Line 9, DEFENDANT STELLAR ENERGY GP INC. Motion To Dismiss Complaint. This action pending the arbitration is stayed pending the conclusion of the arbitration. (Code Of Civil Procedure 128(a)(8) and 1281.4). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or send it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEGALMATCH.COM VS. BRAD S. SAHL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Quash Servcie And Motion To Dismiss Unverified Civil Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction, Affidavit
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 27, 2012, Line 10, DEFENDANT BRAD SAHL Motion To Quash Servcie And Motion To Dismiss Unverified Civil Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction. Denied. Defendant consented to California jurisdiction by entering into an agreement providing that any disputes about the agreement will be resolved in a California court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: TRAMMELL, PHYLLIS et al VS. BALWANTSINH THASKOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 27, 2012, Line 11, DEFENDANT KIRANSINH THAKOR, SHREE JALARAM LODGING LP DBA BUDGET INN, BALWANTSINH THAKOR, THAKOR, LATABAN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first, second, third, fifth, sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth, tenth, twelfth and thirteenth causes of action, sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the class allegations and the fourth and eleventh causes of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the fourteenth cause of action. All causes of action except the fourth, eleventh and fourteenth are adequately pled. Plaintiffs are granted leave to amend to clear up the ambiguity regarding the occupancy time period, plead facts with particularity supporting the UCL claim, and plead facts supporting the alleged disability discrimination. The fourteenth cause of action is duplicative of the first cause of action. Notwithstanding the Court's determination that most of Plaintiffs' causes of action are adequately pled, Plaintiffs are given leave to file the proposed Second Amended Complaint submitted with Plaintiffs' Opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARY PRATER VS. BRENDA KYNE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 27, 2012, Line 13, DEFENDANT BRENDA KYNE, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action. The complaint fails to allege actionable defamation of any kind, and further amendment cannot reasonably cure this defect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARK A CATO VS. COMMUNITY JOB PROGRAM (CJP)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, February 27, 2012, Line 15, DEFENDANT GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Denied. Ms. Free's declaration shows defendant Community Job Program is an unincorporated joint venture or partnership formed by its two venturers/partners, Goodwill and the Private Industry Council, and as such may be sued in its own name. (Code of Civil Procedure 369.5). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
